good: Alternate between jogging and skipping — jog a length, skip a length
Keep effort very low — you should be able to hold a conversation
Breathe through your nose if you can
bad: Going too hard — you're warming the engine, not racing it

good: Face the net, stay low through the hips
Push off the outside foot each time
bad: Crossing your feet over each other

good: Drive each knee up to hip height
Stay on the balls of your feet
bad: Leaning back — stay tall

good: Quick tempo, heels flicking up behind you
Arms pumping in rhythm
bad: Big, slow strides — this should be fast and tight

good: Hands laced behind your head, elbows wide — keep them there throughout
Sit into the squat with your chest proud and knees tracking over your toes
Stand tall at the top, squeeze the glutes
bad: Letting the elbows collapse forward — if they drop, your chest is following
Rushing the descent — control the way down

good: Stand on one leg, hinge forward until your torso and free leg are parallel to the floor
Arms out wide like wings — use them for balance and to open the chest
Squeeze the glute of the standing leg at the top as you return to upright
bad: Letting the hip of the raised leg hike up — keep both hips level
Rushing through — hold a beat at the bottom to feel the balance challenge

good: Standing tall, drive one knee up to hip height then rotate it out to the side
Think 'step over a fence' — full circle through the hip
Hold onto a wall or racket for balance if needed
bad: Leaning away from the working leg — stay upright and let the hip do the work
Small, lazy circles — open the hip as wide as you can each rep

good: Step back and lower until your back knee is just above the floor
Drive that same knee forward and up to hip height as you return to standing
Use the knee drive to load the hip and glute — feel it working
bad: Letting the front knee cave inward — keep it tracking over your second toe
Rushing the drive — the knee lift should be deliberate, not a flick

good: Start small and build to full shoulder range
Slow to fast — let the shoulder open gradually
bad: Going straight to big circles with a cold shoulder

good: Swing across the chest and let momentum carry it back
Alternate arms in a relaxed rhythm
bad: Forcing the range — it should swing freely, not be pulled

good: Take a half-kneeling position — one knee down, one foot forward at 90°
Place both hands behind your head, elbows wide
Rotate your upper back, reaching the top elbow toward the sky
Keep your hips square to the floor — all movement from the thoracic spine
bad: Rotating from the lower back or letting the hips open — lock them in place
Collapsing the chest forward between reps

good: Feet hip-width, bounce through the ankles — minimal knee bend
Think stiff springs, not pogo sticks — fast ground contact
Land softly on the balls of your feet and rebound immediately
bad: Bending too much at the knee — the ankle should be doing the work
Slow, lazy bounces — this should feel snappy

good: Quick dip into a quarter squat, then drive arms up and explode off the floor
Land softly with bent knees, absorbing the force — stick each landing for a beat
Full intent on every rep — this should feel like max effort
bad: Slow, stiff take-off — you want a sharp, fast countermovement
Crashing the landing — control it, don't collapse

good: Start from a split stance, lean forward and drive hard for the first 3–4 steps
Pump the arms aggressively — your legs follow your arms
Stay low for the first few metres, then rise naturally
bad: Standing tall from the start — you need that forward lean to accelerate
Jogging it — these are maximal sprints, not strides
